title: Login with email containing plus-tag and subdomain (RFC-valid but often rejected by naive validators)
description: Verifies that the authentication system correctly accepts RFC 5321–compliant email addresses using the '+' tag syntax and multi-level subdomains. Naive regex validators frequently reject these, locking legitimate users out of their accounts permanently.
expected_result: PASS: User is authenticated and redirected to the post-login dashboard with no errors. FAIL: An inline validation message such as 'Invalid email address' appears despite the email being RFC-compliant, or the server returns a 400/422 error — this would lock out any user who registered with a plus-tag address.

test_steps.expected: Login page is displayed with email and password fields visible

test_steps.action: Fill in the email field with a valid RFC 5321 plus-tag address on a multi-level subdomain
test_steps.expected: The full email string 'qa.lead+smoke_2024@mail.staging.acme.co.uk' appears verbatim in the email field without truncation or modification
Password field accepts input and masks characters
Form is submitted; no inline validation error appears on the email field

test_steps.action: Verify successful authentication and redirect to the authenticated landing page
test_steps.expected: User avatar or authenticated dashboard element is visible, confirming the session was established
